MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-23-2015 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFFS VINFORMATION INC, AND MOUNTAIN VIEW VINTNERS INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT SANDRA GUSTAFSON (FORMERLY KNOWN AS SANDRA DEMARTINI) TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET 2); AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET 2); AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST SAID DEFENDANT AND HER COUNSEL, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART, AS FOLLOWS. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT IN OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS' DISCOVERY MOTION ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO CITE THE EXACT LANGUAGE OF THE DECLARATION TO WHICH THEY RAISE AN OBJECTION. SEE SCHOENDORF V. U.D. REGISTRY, INC. (2002) 97 CAL. APP. 4TH 227, 240; SEE WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2015) 9:102.5(1). FURTHERMORE, THE OBJECTION ARE MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE SANCTIONS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET 2); AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET 2), EXCLUDING #15. DEFENDANT SANDRA GUSTAFSON SHALL PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO EACH OF THESE REQUESTS WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IF A REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS INCLUDED IN A MOTION TO COMPEL, THE NOTICE OF MOTION MUST CONTAIN A REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AND MUST: (1) NAME ALL PARTIES AND ATTORNEYS AGAINST WHOM SANCTIONS ARE BEING SOUGHT; (2) SPECIFY THE TYPE OF SANCTION SOUGHT; (3) CITE THE AUTHORITY FOR SUCH SANCTIONS. CCP 2023.040; SEE WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2015) 8:1194. PLAINTIFFS SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Sandra Gustafson (Formerly Known As Sandra Demartini) To Provide Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 2); And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set 2); And Request For Sanctions Against Said Defendant And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally
Off Calendar Jul-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS VINFORMATION INC, AND MOUNTAIN VIEW VINTNERS INC'S Motion To Compel Defendant Sandra Gustafson (Formerly Known As Sandra Demartini) To Provide Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 2); And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set 2); And Request For Sanctions Against Said Defendant And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally, the motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows. Plaintiffs' Objections to Evidence Submitted by Defendant in Opposition to Plaintiffs' Discovery Motion are OVERRULED. Plaintiffs fail to cite the exact language of the declaration to which they raise an objection. See Schoendorf v. U.D. Registry, Inc. (2002) 97 Cal. App. 4th 227, 240; See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2015) 9:102.5(1). Furthermore, the objection are moot in light of the ruling on the sanctions. The Motion is GRANTED as to all of Special Interrogatories (Set 2); and Request for Production of Documents (Set 2), excluding #15. Defendant Sandra Gustafson shall provide code-compliant, verified responses to each of these requests within two weeks of notice of entry of order. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. If a request for sanctions is included in a motion to compel, the notice of motion must contain a request for sanctions and must: (1) Name all parties and attorneys against whom sanctions are being sought; (2) Specify the type of sanction sought; (3) Cite the authority for such sanctions. CCP 2023.040; See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2015) 8:1194. Plaintiffs shall prepare and submit a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported.
